purpose: Populate Moodie as an emotion-based social film discovery product: people share what a film meant to them, find others who felt similarly about the same films, and discover what those people watched next.
executionOwner: The seed-data implementation owns only deterministic synthetic accounts and records identified by this contract. Seeding remains an explicit operator action.

productFraming.primaryObject: A response to a film, expressed in the viewer's own words and emotional sliders.
productFraming.socialObject: A public response becomes a timeline post. A private response informs only its owner's experience.
productFraming.recommendationPrinciple: Match people by how similarly they responded to the same films, then surface other public films from those people.
productFraming.ratingRule: Moodie does not ask seeded people to rate films. Every active seeded diary rating is null.
productFraming.photoRule: An expression photo is an optional social attachment. It is never evidence that the response was inferred from a face and never changes capture_method from manual. Active uploads accept bounded JPEG, PNG, or WebP data URLs; seed posts use approved app asset paths.

sourceOfTruth.movieValidation: Resolve every film through TMDB and cache the returned film and genre metadata. Never guess IDs or artwork paths.

demoAccess.accountRule: Upsert the demo account in place. Its ID and relationships must survive every seed run.

executionRequirements.transactionSafety: Apply all schema compatibility and seed writes in one transaction and roll back when a required lookup or write fails.
executionRequirements.stableOwnership: Identify users by the exact email manifest and entries by nullable unique seed_key values. Never use domain wildcards as an ownership boundary.
executionRequirements.idempotency: A second run must preserve seed user IDs, seed entry IDs, timestamps, relationships, media, and logical content while creating no duplicates.
executionRequirements.nonSeedSafety: Never delete or rewrite an ordinary user's account, diary, follow, reaction, saved film, or relationship to a seed-owned person or post.
executionRequirements.startupBoundary: Do not seed from API, schema, container, or frontend startup.
executionRequirements.environment: Use the automatically initialized embedded database and require TMDB_API_KEY with a direct failure message.
executionRequirements.completionOutput: Print seed-scoped counts for accounts, films, responses, public posts, optional photos, saved films, follows, and reactions, then instruct the operator to run seed:verify.

requiredCohorts.name: portfolio_demo
requiredCohorts.notes: Give the demo enough private and public history to match with several different people.

requiredCohorts.name: community
requiredCohorts.notes: Each person needs a distinct emotional perspective, not a genre persona or review style.

responseRequirements.rating: Null for every active seeded response.
responseRequirements.watchedOn: Use deterministic dates across the previous 18 months and include repeat viewings for at least two community people.
responseRequirements.noteLength: 20 to 420 characters for every public seed post.
responseRequirements.noteVoice: First-person, specific, emotionally candid, and concerned with what the film meant or stirred in the viewer.
noteProhibitions: craft-scorecard language about acting, editing, cinematography, pacing, plotting, or technical execution
claims that the system diagnosed the viewer
responseRequirements.visibility: The demo is private by default. Every community person has both public and private responses.
responseRequirements.emotionInput: All seeded vectors are reviewed direct input and use capture_method manual with confidence 1.
responseRequirements.expressionImageInput: Active diary writes accept only JPEG, PNG, or WebP base64 data URLs whose decoded bytes match the declared MIME signature and are no larger than 2 MiB. SVG, GIF, external URLs, empty payloads, and mismatched signatures are rejected.
responseRequirements.emotionVector: All seven values are between 0 and 1 and need not sum to 1. Avoid uniform or one-hot vectors.
responseRequirements.emotionalCoverage: Every emotion is dominant in multiple responses. Each person has recurring feelings plus credible outliers.

socialRequirements.bios: Write 40 to 160 characters in plain first-person language about what tends to stay with that person after a film.
socialRequirements.sharedFilmOverlap: The demo shares at least three films with at least six community people.
socialRequirements.matchedPeople: At least three people closely resemble the demo across shared films and at least two respond differently, demonstrating that feelings are personal.
socialRequirements.candidateDepth: Every closely matched person has at least three public films the demo has not watched.
socialRequirements.follows: Use a sparse directed graph. The demo follows three people; community people follow one to four others; no self-follows.
socialRequirements.reactions: React only to public seed posts, with zero to five reactions per post and at least 25 percent with none.
socialRequirements.feedDiversity: The authenticated demo feed's first 24 posts includes at least six people and twelve films, with followed people appearing before the wider community.
socialRequirements.photoPosts: Attach the three approved generated expression assets to their named public posts. Store them as entry_media kind expression_photo; keep capture_method manual.

recommendationCoverage.matchingBasis: Emotional similarity on films both people watched.
recommendationCoverage.candidateBasis: Other public responses from emotionally matched people.
recommendationCoverage.privacy: The viewer's public and private responses may inform their own match. Another person's private responses never become timeline content or recommendation candidates.
recommendationCoverage.excludedMovieRule: Never recommend a film already present in the viewer's responses.
recommendationCoverage.payloadRule: Recommendation candidates identify the people behind them. Feed and recommendation framing must not expose diary ratings, TMDB votes, top genres, or genre/rating reasons.

prohibited: Do not call legacy user-movie, emotion-mapping, profile, or recommendations tables.
Do not make every response public.
Do not treat an expression photo as emotion evidence.
Do not attach a photo to a private post.
Do not use real camera frames or real-person uploads as seed media.
Do not rank recommendations by ratings, TMDB vote, popularity, or genre affinity.
Do not delete and recreate seed users or seed posts during reconciliation.
Do not scan or mutate unrelated user content during verification.
acceptanceChecks: Demo login returns a valid token.
Every active seeded rating is null and every capture_method is manual.
Every public seeded note is first-person and passes the emotional-response language gate.
The demo shares at least three films with at least six people.
The first 24 authenticated feed posts contain at least six people and twelve films and omit rating and vote_average fields.
The three approved expression assets appear on the intended public posts and do not change capture_method.
People results use same-film emotional overlap.
Recommendations are non-empty, exclude watched films, identify matched people, and contain no genre/rating reasons.
Private responses do not appear in discovery payloads.
A second seed run preserves seed user IDs, entry IDs, timestamps, and fingerprints.
A randomized non-seed account, diary entry, follow to a seed person, and reaction to a seed post survive a seed rerun unchanged.
